hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic and hydrophobic interactions at position 5 and 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by better complementing SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5/TM6 residues, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues will disrupt off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective for generating strong SSTR2 binders but insufficient for achieving robust selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the critical FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14 pharmacophore.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining the overall scaffold and pharmacophore integrity.
